title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard from Google Chrome that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured, named tools rather than DOM parsing and pixel coordinate clicks. It provides both declarative API (HTML attributes) and imperative API (JavaScript registration) for defining agent-callable actions, with early preview support in Chrome 146.
writeup: This talk introduces WebMCP, a proposed web standard from Google that lets AI agents interact with web services through structured, typed tools rather than visual scraping. It matters because it shifts agent-to-web interaction from brittle page parsing toward explicit, registered actions, drawing on the Model Context Protocol. The concrete takeaways: WebMCP exposes both a declarative HTML-based API and an imperative JavaScript API for registering actions, and it is currently in early preview on Chrome 146.
